titleStmt.title: SII 25.259: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 259. (A.R. No. 259 of 1908.) TĒMARĀ, BASTAR STATE, CENTRAL PROVINCES.

summary.p: On a sati-stone found in the temple ruins.
This buried stone inscription is in Sanskrit language and Nāgarī characters. It may palaeographically be assigned to c. 14th century.

The record is dated in the Śaka year 1246, Raktākshi, Chaitra śu [12], śanau (Saturday). The cyclic year Raktākshi fell in the Śaka year 1247, which was current. The tithi dvādaśi does not coincide with the month Chaitra and the week-day Saturday. Therefore, the tithi was navami on that day. However, the details of date work out to Saturday, the 23rd March, 1325 A.D. It records the immolation of the Māṇikyadēvī after the death of her husband at Ṭēmarā-sthāna of Sairaharāja-rājya in Chakrakūṭa-rāshṭra.

The epigraph does not give any clue as to what family king Hariśchaṁdra belonged. But it is not impossible that he belonged to the Chhinda or Sinda family of the Nāga race who ruled during the 11th12th centuries. The reasons for this inference are: 1. Chakrakūṭa was situated in Bastar and the fact that Ṭēmrā is a village adjoining Kuruspāl being included in its rāshṭra (i.e., Chakrakūṭa-rāshṭra) during the reign of king Hariśchandra. and 2. The name of his (kings) wife Māṇikyadēvī was so named after the goddess Māṇikyadēvī, worshipped by the kings of Nāgavaṁśi family. This is an ancient name of Dantēśvarī so named by the successors of the Nāgavaṁśis, the Kākatīyas. The Bhairamgārh inscription contains the birudas similar to those found in the Bārsūr one, and the king is stated in both to be the worshipper of Māṇikyadēvī (śrī-Māṇikyadēvī-divya-śrīpāda-padm-ārādhaka) (Ep. Ind., Vol. IX p. 164; Ibid., Vol. III, p. 318). The Nāgavaṁśi rulers seem to have worshipped Durgā under the name Māṇikyadēvī (Ep. Ind., Vol. X. p. 27, foot note 7).
It mentions the glorious (god) Kalaṁkāṁ Nārāyaṇa residing at Mahāgōshṭha and Āmaṇa, the executive officer (dēśakāri) of Hariśchaṁdradēva.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī cakrakoṭa rāṣṭra
śrī sairaharāja rājye hema
rā stāne mahāgoṣṭha samā
vāsa śrī kalaṁkāṁ nārāyaṇa
prekṣita satya vakrā satya guruḥ
rājaśrī hariścaṁdradeva rājyeḥ
sake 1246 raktakṣi saṁvatsareha
caitra sudi 12 śanau śrī hariścaṁdra
deva Ādeśakārī Āmaṇa sva
rṇa loke gate tat sakī satī
śrī māṇikyadve Agnau pravi
śya kalpāntam
ravi lokā
. ḥ .
Digital edition of SII 25.259 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
